prompt: Japanese Edo-period ukiyo-e woodblock print, circa 1830s–1860s Hokusai/Hiroshige school. A flat-colour illustration depicting scattered data points clustered into three groups across a landscape composition — rendered as scattered stones, flowers, or travellers naturally gathering into formations along a misty mountain path. Visible woodgrain texture in background areas. Stark figure-ground contrast with strong black brushstroke outlines (keyblock lines). Flat, unmodulated areas of colour — muted indigo blue, ochre yellow, vermillion red, and ivory paper-white. No photographic shading, no gradients, no Western perspective vanishing points. Atmospheric depth achieved through vertical layering and colour saturation, not shadow. Diagonal compositional thrust from lower-left to upper-right. Fine parallel hatching lines for texture in rock and sky areas. Small cartouche box in upper corner containing title text in the style of Japanese characters
tradition_approach: Utilized flat-color woodblock illustration and strong black keyblock outlines to represent data clusters as natural gatherings of travelers and scholars. Atmospheric depth was achieved through vertical layering and muted indigo and vermillion pigments, replacing Western perspective with a diagonal compositional thrust
